name: Sour Cream Chocolate Layer Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt into a large mixing bowl.
2. Add shortening, sour cream, vanilla, eggs, water, and melted and cooled chocolate. Beat with an electric mixer at low speed until combined.
3. Increase speed to high and beat for 1/2 minute, scraping the bowl occasionally.
4. Pour batter evenly into two greased and waxed-paper lined 9-inch round cake pans.
5. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for 35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
6. Cool in the pans on racks for 10 minutes.
7. Remove from the pans and cool completely on racks.
8. Place one cake layer on a serving plate. Spread with sour cream/chocolate frosting.
9. Top with the second cake layer, spreading frosting on the sides and top of the cake.
10. For best results, trim any uneven tops of the layers to be flat before frosting between layers and placing the other layer right side up. This creates the illusion of a single domed layer and ensures stability on the plate.
